props.text: The AI Industry Is Racing to Build Smarter Models. The Compounding Plays Are Building Citation-Grade Outputs.

The EU AI Act's auditable-output rules hit August 2026 and a wave of arXiv papers (PROV-AGENT, MAIF) are converging on the same idea: AI's next moat isn't reasoning, it's record-keeping. MiroShark spent the last seven days shipping that thesis in PRs — graph memory, gallery, MCP, OpenAPI, webhook, /verified, replay GIF, and today's PR #57 transcript export (Markdown + YAML front matter that Notion, Obsidian, Bear, and Substack pick up natively). The non-obvious move: every surface reads from the same on-disk files, so the artifact can't lie about itself. The article argues this is the compounding bet the loud benchmark-discourse is missing.

props.title: The AI Industry Is Racing to Build Smarter Models. The Compounding Plays Are Building Citation-Grade Outputs.
